LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF GEODE INVESTMENTS PRIME, LLC'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION AS TO FIRST CAUSE OF ACTION FOR BREACH OF CONTRACT AGAINST DEFENDANT ULTRATICK, INC. PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION ORIGINALLY CAME ON FOR HEARING ON FEBRUARY 19, 2019. THE MOTION SEEKS SUMMARY ADJUDICATION AGAINST DEFENDANT ULTRATICK, INC. AND DOES NOT SEEK RELIEF AGAINST ITS MANAGER SEAN SHARMA. THE COURT TENTATIVELY TOOK THE MOTION OFF CALENDAR BECAUSE PLAINTIFF FILED A NOTICE OF SETTLEMENT ON JANUARY 14, 2019. PLAINTIFF AND DEFENDANT'S FORMER COUNSEL APPEARED AT THE HEARING AND EXPLAINED THAT MR. SHARMA SUDDENLY PASSED AWAY BEFORE THE SETTLEMENT COULD BE FINALIZED. THE COURT CONTINUED THE MOTION TO ALLOW THE PARTIES TO LOCATE A SUCCESSOR IN INTEREST WHO COULD EFFECTUATE THE SETTLEMENT. THE COURT HAS BEEN INFORMED THAT MR. SHARMA LIKELY HAS NO NEXT OF KIN. THE COURT ALSO LEARNED THAT IT IS UNCLEAR WHO CAN ACT FOR ULTRATICK, INC., WHICH IS NOW ACTING IN PRO PER BASED ON THE SUBSTITUTION OF COUNSEL FILED ON FEBRUARY 1, 2019. THE COURT CONTINUES THIS MOTION TO APRIL 30, 2019, TO GIVE ULTRATICK, INC. AN OPPORTUNITY TO OBTAIN NEW COUNSEL AND DETERMINE WHETHER IT INTENDS TO OPPOSE THESE MOTIONS OR FINALIZE THE SETTLEMENT. PLAINTIFF'S COUNSEL SHALL GIVE NOTICE OF THE CONTINUANCE TO ULTRATICK'S CORPORATE COUNSEL (TARAK SHAH) AND FORMER COUNSEL (ANDREW LEVINE). ULTRATICK WILL HAVE UNTIL APRIL 16, 2019 TO FILE ANY OPPOSITION TO THIS MOTION IF IT WISHES TO DO SO. IF NO OPPOSITION IS FILED, THE MOTION WILL BE GRANTED AS UNOPPOSED ON APRIL 30, 2019. JUDGE: ETHAN P. SCHULMAN, CLERK: S. LE, NOT REPORTED. =(302/EPS)

LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF GEODE INVESTMENTS BETA, LLC'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION AS TO SECOND CAUSE OF ACTION FOR BREACH OF CONTRACT AGAINST DEFENDANT ULTRATICK, INC. PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION ORIGINALLY CAME ON FOR HEARING ON FEBRUARY 19, 2019. THE MOTION SEEKS SUMMARY ADJUDICATION AGAINST DEFENDANT ULTRATICK, INC. AND DOES NOT SEEK RELIEF AGAINST ITS MANAGER SEAN SHARMA. THE COURT TENTATIVELY TOOK THE MOTION OFF CALENDAR BECAUSE PLAINTIFF FILED A NOTICE OF SETTLEMENT ON JANUARY 14, 2019. PLAINTIFF AND DEFENDANT'S FORMER COUNSEL APPEARED AT THE HEARING AND EXPLAINED THAT MR. SHARMA SUDDENLY PASSED AWAY BEFORE THE SETTLEMENT COULD BE FINALIZED. THE COURT CONTINUED THE MOTION TO ALLOW THE PARTIES TO LOCATE A SUCCESSOR IN INTEREST WHO COULD EFFECTUATE THE SETTLEMENT. THE COURT HAS BEEN INFORMED THAT MR. SHARMA LIKELY HAS NO NEXT OF KIN. THE COURT ALSO LEARNED THAT IT IS UNCLEAR WHO CAN ACT FOR ULTRATICK, INC., WHICH IS NOW ACTING IN PRO PER BASED ON THE SUBSTITUTION OF COUNSEL FILED ON FEBRUARY 1, 2019. THE COURT CONTINUES THIS MOTION TO APRIL 30, 2019, TO GIVE ULTRATICK, INC. AN OPPORTUNITY TO OBTAIN NEW COUNSEL AND DETERMINE WHETHER IT INTENDS TO OPPOSE THESE MOTIONS OR FINALIZE THE SETTLEMENT. PLAINTIFF'S COUNSEL SHALL GIVE NOTICE OF THE CONTINUANCE TO ULTRATICK'S CORPORATE COUNSEL (TARAK SHAH) AND FORMER COUNSEL (ANDREW LEVINE). ULTRATICK WILL HAVE UNTIL APRIL 16, 2019 TO FILE ANY OPPOSITION TO THIS MOTION IF IT WISHES TO DO SO. IF NO OPPOSITION IS FILED, THE MOTION WILL BE GRANTED AS UNOPPOSED ON APRIL 30, 2019. JUDGE: ETHAN P. SCHULMAN, CLERK: S. LE, NOT REPORTED. =(302/EPS)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION As To First Cause Of Action For Breach Of Contract Against Defendant Ultratick, Inc.
Off Calendar Mar-18-2019 Continued To Apr-30-19 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:

No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF GEODE INVESTMENTS PRIME, LLC's MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION As To First Cause Of Action For Breach Of Contract Against Defendant Ultratick, Inc.

Plaintiff's motion for summary adjudication originally came on for hearing on February 19, 2019. The motion seeks summary adjudication against defendant Ultratick, Inc. and does not seek relief against its manager Sean Sharma. The court tentatively took the motion off calendar because plaintiff filed a notice of settlement on January 14, 2019. Plaintiff and defendant's former counsel appeared at the hearing and explained that Mr. Sharma suddenly passed away before the settlement could be finalized. The court continued the motion to allow the parties to locate a successor in interest who could effectuate the settlement. The court has been informed that Mr. Sharma likely has no next of kin. The court also learned that it is unclear who can act for Ultratick, Inc., which is now acting in pro per based on the substitution of counsel filed on February 1, 2019.

The court continues this motion to April 30, 2019, to give Ultratick, Inc. an opportunity to obtain new counsel and determine whether it intends to oppose these motions or finalize the settlement. Plaintiff's counsel shall give notice of the continuance to Ultratick's corporate counsel (Tarak Shah) and former counsel (Andrew Levine). Ultratick will have until April 16, 2019 to file any opposition to this motion if it wishes to do so. If no opposition is filed, the motion will be granted as unopposed on April 30, 2019.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: S. Le, Not Reported. =(302/EPS)
